mission: Freeze the exact Phase 1 intake event set so browser, embedded, questionnaire, attachment, and urgent-diversion follow-on work cannot fork event meaning.
source_precedence: blueprint/phase-1-the-red-flag-gate.md#1A. Journey contract and intake schema lock
blueprint/phase-1-the-red-flag-gate.md#1E. Submit normalization and synchronous safety engine
blueprint/phase-1-the-red-flag-gate.md#1F. Triage handoff, receipt, ETA, and minimal status tracking

forbiddenSemanticDuplicates.reason: request.submitted is the only canonical submit event for the durable envelope-to-request promotion boundary.

gap_resolutions.summary: The public journey now has one exact Phase 1 path set with stable route patterns, outcome paths, and recovery paths instead of narrative-only intent.
Browser, embedded, and future authenticated uplift all resolve through one IntakeConvergenceContract and one IntakeDraftView schema.
Receipt and status visibility now require IntakeSubmitSettlement plus the SubmissionPromotionRecord chain rather than local browser success inference.
Receipt, urgent guidance, and status surfaces are bound to ArtifactPresentationContract and OutboundNavigationGrant instead of raw URLs or detached pages.
Urgent diversion, stale recovery, and failed-safe outcomes remain in one same-shell lineage instead of collapsing to generic expired-link or error pages.

bounded_gaps.summary: Question semantics, per-type field rules, and questionnaire decision tables are deferred to seq_140 without changing this route or schema spine.
Attachment media acceptance, classification, and quarantine policy detail is frozen only at the event and API seam here; seq_141 hardens the rulebook.
Urgent-diversion wording and the rules-first red-flag metadata pack remain bounded follow-on work in seq_142.
Embedded posture is modeled as a future-compatible blocked seam so later NHS App work cannot fork the data model or promotion boundary.
Authenticated track-my-request uplift stays reserved for later route publication work and may narrow chrome only, not semantics.

eventCatalog.emittedWhen: The durable envelope-to-request promotion boundary commits one SubmissionPromotionRecord and Request.workflowState = submitted.
eventCatalog.patientVisibleMeaning: Submit is authoritative only after this event and the matching IntakeSubmitSettlement chain settle.
sourceRefs: blueprint/phase-1-the-red-flag-gate.md#1A. Journey contract and intake schema lock
blueprint/phase-1-the-red-flag-gate.md#1E. Submit normalization and synchronous safety engine

eventCatalog.emittedWhen: The canonical draft lineage is opened for self-service intake.
eventCatalog.patientVisibleMeaning: A resume token and a draft public ID now exist for the same envelope lineage.
sourceRefs: blueprint/phase-1-the-red-flag-gate.md#1A. Journey contract and intake schema lock

eventCatalog.emittedWhen: An immutable DraftMutationRecord and corresponding draft projection update settle.
eventCatalog.patientVisibleMeaning: Quiet saved posture may change only when continuity evidence still covers the same shell lineage.

eventCatalog.emittedWhen: A governed attachment placeholder or initiated upload is bound to the same draft lineage.
eventCatalog.patientVisibleMeaning: Supporting files are part of the same draft summary and replay boundary.
sourceRefs: blueprint/phase-1-the-red-flag-gate.md#1A. Journey contract and intake schema lock

eventCatalog.emittedWhen: Attachment acceptance or quarantine law forces a bounded non-live attachment path on the same lineage.
eventCatalog.patientVisibleMeaning: Seq_141 must harden this rule without renaming the event.

eventCatalog.emittedWhen: The frozen submission snapshot yields one canonical NormalizedSubmission after durable submitted state exists.
eventCatalog.patientVisibleMeaning: Routine queue entry and ETA proof happen after this normalization boundary, not before.
sourceRefs: blueprint/phase-1-the-red-flag-gate.md#1E. Submit normalization and synchronous safety engine

eventCatalog.emittedWhen: The synchronous safety engine evaluates the canonical submission snapshot.
eventCatalog.patientVisibleMeaning: Urgent, routine, and failed-safe outcomes all trace to one rules-first safety record set.
sourceRefs: blueprint/phase-1-the-red-flag-gate.md#1E. Submit normalization and synchronous safety engine

eventCatalog.emittedWhen: The safety engine crosses the durable urgent-required boundary before the urgent guidance settlement completes.
eventCatalog.patientVisibleMeaning: Urgent required and urgent diverted remain separate states; the UI may not collapse them.
sourceRefs: blueprint/phase-1-the-red-flag-gate.md#1E. Submit normalization and synchronous safety engine

eventCatalog.emittedWhen: One UrgentDiversionSettlement is durably issued for the same request lineage.
eventCatalog.patientVisibleMeaning: The urgent pathway is now settled and may present governed external handoff if policy requires it.
sourceRefs: blueprint/phase-1-the-red-flag-gate.md#1E. Submit normalization and synchronous safety engine

eventCatalog.emittedWhen: A non-urgent request becomes triage_ready with one authoritative task.
eventCatalog.patientVisibleMeaning: Receipt and ETA may render only after this downstream operational handoff exists.
sourceRefs: blueprint/phase-1-the-red-flag-gate.md#1F. Triage handoff, receipt, ETA, and minimal status tracking

eventCatalog.emittedWhen: The patient-facing receipt projection settles under the active IntakeSurfaceRuntimeBinding.
eventCatalog.patientVisibleMeaning: Receipt, status, and later authenticated views stay semantically equivalent for the same request.
sourceRefs: blueprint/phase-1-the-red-flag-gate.md#1F. Triage handoff, receipt, ETA, and minimal status tracking

eventCatalog.emittedWhen: Receipt or follow-up communication is queued after the request is durably operational.
eventCatalog.patientVisibleMeaning: Communication is a downstream effect of authoritative receipt, not a substitute for it.
sourceRefs: blueprint/phase-1-the-red-flag-gate.md#1F. Triage handoff, receipt, ETA, and minimal status tracking
